entry.title: 《Deep Learning》 Reading Nodes:3
entry.content: Linear AlgebraIt about eigendecomposition, singular value decomposition, the Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse, the trace operator and the determinant. Eigendecomposition(特征分解)One of the most widely used kinds of matrix decomposition(分解) is called eigendecomposition, in which we decompose(分解) a matrix into a set of eigenvectors(特征向量) and eigenvalues(特征值). An eigenvector of a square matrix A is a nonzero vector v such that multiplication(乘法) by A alters(改变) only the scale(缩放) of v : The salar λ is know as the eigenvector corresponding(对应的) to this eigenvector. (One can also find a left eigenvector such that vT A = λ v, but we are usually concerned(关注) with right eifenvectors) We usually look only for unit(基础的) eigenvetors. Suppose that a matrix A has n linearly independent eigenvectors {v(1), …, v(n)} with corresponding eigenvalues{λ1,…,λn}.We may concatenate(把…联系起来) all the eigenvectors to form a matrix V with one eigenvector per column(列): V = [v(1), …, v(n)]. Likewise(同样地), we can concatenate the eigenvalues to form a vector λ = [λ1,…,λn]. The eigendecomposition of A is then given by Not every matrix can be decomposed into eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Specifically, every real symmetric matrix can be decomposed into an expression using only real-valued eigenvectors and eigenvalues: where Q is an orthogonal(正交的) matrix composed(组成) of eigenvectors of A, and Λ is a diagonal(对角) matrix. The eigenvalue Λ i,i is associated(对应) with the eigenvector in column i of Q, denoted(记做) as Q :,i. Because Q is an orthogonal matrix, we can think of A as scalling space by λ i in direction(方向) v(i). While any real symmetric matrix A is guaranteed(都有) to have an eigendecomposition, the eigendecomposition may not be unique(唯一的). If any two or more eigenvectors share the same eigenvalue, then any set of orthogonal vectors lying in their spanare also eigenvectors with that eigenvalue, and we could equivalently choose a Q using those eigenvectors instead(代替). By convention(约定), we usually sort(将…排序) the entries of Λ in descending(递减) order. Under this convention, the eigendecomposition is unique only if all the eigenvalues are unique. A matrix whose eigenvalues are all positive is called positive definite(正定). A matrix whose eigenvalues are all positive or zero valued is called positive semidefinite(半正定). Likewise, if all eigenvalues are negative, the matrix is negative definite(负定), and if all eigenvalues are negative or zero valued, it is negative semidefinite(半负定). Positive semidefinite matrices are interesting because they guarantee(保证) that ∀ x, x TAx ≥ 0. Positive definite matrices additionally(此外) guarantee that x TAx = 0 ⇒ x = 0. Singular Value Decomposition(奇异值分解)The Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) provides another way to factorize a matric, into singular vactors(奇异向量) and singular values(奇异值). Every real matrix has a singular value decomposition, but the same is not true of the eigenvalue decomposition. The singular value decomposition id similar, except this time we will write A as a product of three matrices: Suppose that A is an m n matrix. Then **U is deﬁned to be an m m matrix, *D to be an m n matrix, and *V** to be an n n matrix. Each of these matrices is deﬁned to have a special structure(结构). The matrices U and V are both deﬁned to be orthogonal(正教的) matrices. The matrix D is deﬁned to be a diagonal matrix. Note that D is not necessarily square. The elements along the diagonal of D are known as the singular values(奇异值) ofthe matrix A. The columns of U are known as the left-singular vectors(左奇异向量). The columns of V are known as the right-singular vectors(右奇异向量). The left-singular vectors of A are the eigenvectors of AAT. The right-singular of A are the eigenvectors of AT A. The nonzero singular values of A are the square roots of the eigenvalues of AT A. The same is true for AAT. The Moore-Penrose Pseudoinverse(Moore-Penrose 伪逆)The Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse enables us to make some headway in not square matrix to solve a linear equation like: The pseudoinverse of A is defined as a matrix Practical(实际的) algorithms(运算法则) for computing the pseudoinverse(计算伪逆) are based not on this definition, but rather on the formula(准则)where U, D and V are the singular value decomposition of A, and the pseudoinverse D+ of a diagonal matrix D is obtained(获得) by taking the reciprocal(倒数) of its nonzero elements then taking the transpose of the resulting matrix. When A has more columns than rows, then solving a linear equation using the pseudoinverse provides one of the many possible solutions. Specifically, it provides the solution x = A+y with minimal Euclidean norm(欧几里得范数) ||x||2 among all possible solutions. When A has more rows than columns, it is possible for there to be no solution. In this case, using the pseudoinverse gives us the x for which Ax is as close as possible to y in terms of Euclidean norm || Ax - y ||2. The Trace Operator(迹运算)The trace operator gives the sum of all the diagonal entries of a matrix: The trace operator provides an alternative(代替的) way of writing the Frobenius norm of a matrix: The trace operator is invariant(不变的) to the transpose operator: The trace of a square matrix composed(组成) of many factors(因素) is also invariant to moving the last factor into the frist position(位置), if the shapes of the corresponding(相应的) matries allow the resulting product to be defined: or more generally, This invariance to cyclic permutation holds even if the resulting product has a different shape.For example, for A ∈ Rm n and **B* ∈ R*m n. Another useful to keep in mind is that a scalar(标量) is its own trace: a = Tr(a). The Determinant(行列式)The determinant of a square matrix, denoted det(A), is a function that maps matrices to real scalars. The determinant is equal to the product of all the eigenvalues of the matrix. The determinant can be thought of as a measure(测量) of how much multiplocation(增加) by the matrix expands or contracts(收缩) space.

entry.title: 《Deep Learning》 Reading Nodes:2
entry.content: Linear AlgebraIt about norms and special kinds of matrices and vectors. Norms(范数)In machine learning, we usually measure(评估) the size of vectors using a function called a norm(范数). Formally, the Lp norm is given by for p ∈ R, p ≥ 1. Norms, include the Lp norm, are functions mapping(映射) vectors to non-negative(非负) values. On an intuitive level(直观来说), the norm of a vector x measure(测量) the distance(距离) from the origin to point x. More rigorously(严格的), a norm is any function f that satisfies(满足) the following properties(): f (x) = 0 ⇒ x = 0 f (x + y) ≤ f (x) + f (y)(the triangle inequality) ∀ α ∈ R, f (αx) = |α|f (x) The L2 norm, with p = 2, is known as the Euclidean norm(欧几里得范数), which is simply the Euclidean distance from the origin to the point identified by x. It is also common to measure(测量) the size of a vector using the squared L2 norm, which can be calculates(计算) as xTx. We sometimes measure the size of the vector by counting its number of nonzero elements. The number of nonzero entries in a vector is not a norm, because scaling(缩放) the vector by α does not change the number of nonzero entries. The L1 norm is oftem used as a substitute(代替) for the number of nonzero entries. Every time an element of x moves away from 0 by ε, the L1 norm increases(增加) by ε. One other norm that commonly arises(出现) in marchine learning is the L∞ norm, also known as the max norm(最大范数) . This norm simplifies to the absolute value of the element with the largest magnitude(幅值) in the vector, Sometimes we also wish to measure the size of a matrix. In the context of deep learning, the most common way to do this is with the otherwise obscure(隐藏的) Frobenius norm: The dot product of two vectors can bu rewritten in terms of norms. Specifically,Where θ is the angle between x and y. Special Kinds of Matrices and VectorsDiagonal matrices(对角矩阵) consist mostly of zeros and have nonzero entries only along the main diagonal. We write diag(v) to denote a square diagonal matrix whose diagonal entries are given by the entries of the vector v. Not all diagonal matrices need be a square. It is possible to construct(构建) a rectangular(长方形) diagonal matrix. Nonsquare diagonal matrices do not have inverses(逆). A symmetric matrix(对称矩阵) is any matrix that is equal to its own transpose: A unit vector(单位向量) is a vector with unit norm(单位范数): A vector x and a vector y are orthogonal(正交) to each other if xTy = 0. If both vectors have nonzero norm, this means that they are at a 90 degree angle to each other. In Rn, at most n vectors not only are orthogonal but also have unit norm, we call them orthonormal(标准正交). An orthogonal matrix(正交矩阵) is a square matrix whose rows are mutually(相互) orthonormal and whose columns are mutually orthonormal:This implies(表明) that

entry.title: 《Deep Learning》 Reading Nodes:1
entry.content: Linear Algebra(线性代数)Linear algebra is a branch(科) of mathematics(数学) that is widely used throughout science and engineering(工程). Scalars, Vectos, Matrices, Tensor, Identity and Inverse Matrices, Mutiplying Matrices and Vsctors, Linear Dependence and Span Scalars, Vectos, Matrices and Tensor Scalars(标量): A scalars means a single number. Vectos(向量): A vector id an array. The number are arranged in order.We wirte them as a column(列) enclosed in square brackets(方括号): Matrices(矩阵): A matrix is a 2-D array of number,so each is identified(可识别的) by two indices(标记) instead of just one.We write them as an array enclosed in square brackets: Tensors(张量): In the general case, an array of numbers arranged(安排) on a regular(规则的) grid(网格) with a variable(可变的) number of axes(轴) is known as a tensor. Transpose(转置) is one important operation(运算) on matrices. The transpose of a matrix is the mirror(镜子) image of the matrix across a diagonal(对角线) line, called the main diagonal(主对角线), running down and to the right, starting from its upper left corner. We denote the trandpose of matrix A as AT, and it is define such that Vectors(向量) can be thought of as matrices that contain(b包含) only one column. The transpose of a vector is therefore a matrix with only one row. Like x = [x1,x2,x3]T A scalar can be thought of as a matrix with only a single entry. A scalar is its own transpose: a = aT. The matrices can be added to each other, as long as they have same shape, just by adding their corresponding(对应的) elements: C = A + B where Ci,j = Ai,j + Bi,j. A scalar can add or mutiply a matrix by a scalar, just by performing(执行) that operation(运算) on each element of a matrix: D = a · B + c where Di,j = a · Bi,j + c. In deep learning, we allow the addition of matrix and a vector, yielding another matrix: C = A + b, where Ci,j = Ai,j + Bj.This implicit(隐式的) copying of b to many locations is called broadcastion(广播). Mutiplying Matrices and VsctorsThe matrix product(矩阵乘积) of matrices A and B is a third matrix C. A must have the same number of columns as B has rows. If A is of shape m n and **B is of shape n p. We can write the matrix product like *C = AB***. The product operation(运算) is defined by The product of two matrices is just a matrix containing(包含) the product of the individual(个别的) elements calles the element-wise product(元素对应乘积), or Hadamard product, and is denoted as A ⊙ B. The dot product between two vectors x and y of the same dimensionality(维度) is the matrix product xTy Identity and Inverse Matrices(单位矩阵和逆矩阵)The structure(结构) of the identity matrix(单位矩阵) is: all the entries(元素) along the main diagonal(对角线) are 1, while all the other entries are zero. The matrix inverse(逆矩阵) of A is denoted as A-1, and it is defined as the matrix such that: Now we can solve Ax = b follwing this: Linear Dependence and SpanFor Ax think of A as specifying(指定) different directions(方向) we can travel(移动) in from the origin(原点) (the point specified by the vector of all zeros), then determine(决定) how many ways there are of reaching b. In this view, each element of x specifying how far we should travel in each of these directions, with xi specifying how far to move in the direction of column i: This kind of operation is calles a linear combination(线性组合).Formally(形式上), a linear combination of some set of vectors {v(1), . . . ,v(n)} is given by multiplying each vector v(n) by a corresponding(相应的) scalar(标量) coefficient(系数) and adding the results: The span(生成子空间) of a set of vectors is the set of all points obtainable(可获得的) by linear combination of the original vectors. Determining(决定) wheter Ax = b has a solution thus amounts to testing whether b is in the span of the columns of A. This particular span is known as the column space(列空间), or the range(值域), of A. In order for the system Ax = b to have a solution for all values of b ∈ Rm.The requirement that the column space of A be all of Rm implies(暗示) immediately that A must have at least m columns, that is, n ≥ m. Otherwise, the dimensionality(维度) of the column space would be less than m. Having n ≥ m is only a necessary condition(条件) for every point to have a solution. It is not a suﬃcient(充分) condition, because it is possible for some of the columns to be redundant(冗余).Formally, this kind of redundancy is known as linear dependence(线性相关). A set of vectors is linearly independent(线性无关) if no vector in the set is a linear combination of the other vectors. A square(方阵) matrix with linearly dependent columns is known as singular(奇异的).
